name: Guadiana Natural Trail
name_original: Castillo de Calatrava la Vieja por la Ermita de la Virgen de la Encarnación — circular por el Río Guadiana
description: Hike the moderate 6.4-mile Guadiana Natural Trail, gaining 127 feet over 2 hours and 38 minutes, with historical sites and river views.
region_intro: Hiking around Carrión De Calatrava offers diverse landscapes, from riverine paths along the Río Guadiana to the hilly terrain of the Sierra de Malagón. The region features natural reservoirs like Embalse de Vicario and Embalse de Gasset, integrated into various hiking routes. Hikers can explore wooded pastures (Dehesas) and holm oak forests (Encinares), alongside historical sites such as the Castillo de Calatrava la Vieja. This blend provides varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails in Carrión De Calatrava The most popular hiking route is Embalse de Vicario by the Baños del Emperador or Trujillo — circular by the Río Guadiana, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete, offering waterside scenery around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Path from Fernán Caballero to El Congosto — circular by the Embalse de Gasset, a moderate 8.4 miles (13.6 km) path. This route provides views of the Embalse de Gasset and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the Ascent to the Plaza de los Moros via the Sierra de Malagón — circular from Malagón, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Malagón, often completed in about 6 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Carrión De Calatrava is defined by river paths, reservoir circuits, and historical sites like the Castillo de Calatrava la Vieja. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Carrión De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Carrión De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Carrión De Calatrava?
A: There are over 130 hiking routes in Carrión De Calatrava, catering to various skill levels. This includes 65 easy trails, 67 moderate, and 4 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Carrión De Calatrava offers numerous easy hiking options. Many trails follow riverine paths along the Río Guadiana or gentle circuits around reservoirs like the Embalse de Vicario. An example of a moderate, yet accessible, route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201502677/embalse-de-vicario-by-the-banos-del-emperador-or-trujillo-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Vicario by the Baños del Emperador or Trujillo — circular by the Río Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, which offers picturesque waterside scenery.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes around Carrión De Calatrava?
A: The region boasts diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverine paths along the Río Guadiana to the hilly terrain of the Sierra de Malagón. You'll encounter natural reservoirs like Embalse de Vicario and Embalse de Gasset, wooded pastures (Dehesas), and holm oak forests (Encinares). The Peralvillo region is particularly notable for its protected wetlands, indigenous vegetation, and varied geomorphologies.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many trails in Carrión De Calatrava are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1205933375/path-from-fernan-caballero-to-el-congosto-circular-by-the-embalse-de-gasset" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path from Fernán Caballero to El Congosto — circular by the Embalse de Gasset&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Carrión De Calatrava?
A: While many trails in natural areas are generally dog-friendly, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Please be mindful of local regulations and ensure you clean up after your pet to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore while hiking?
A: Carrión De Calatrava is rich in history. You can hike to the impressive ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.spain.info/en/places-of-interest/archaeological-park-calatrava-la-vieja/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Calatrava la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient fortified city. Other notable sites include the Ermita de la Virgen de la Encarnación, the Roman-origin Puente de Malvecinos, and the Puente de Hierro, a former railway bridge now part of the Route of Don Quixote. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201494099/castillo-de-calatrava-la-vieja-by-the-ermita-de-la-virgen-de-la-encarnacion-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Calatrava la Vieja by the Ermita de la Virgen de la Encarnación — circular by the Río Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; route combines historical exploration with riverside scenery.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting?
A: Yes, the region is home to diverse wildlife. The protected wetland area in Peralvillo is crucial for aquatic birds like coots, cattle egrets, and red-crested pochards. You might also spot various reptiles, including Montpellier snakes and different types of lizards, as well as the European pond turtle, especially near water bodies and in the Dehesas and Encinares.

Q: What are some more challenging hikes in Carrión De Calatrava?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the Sierra de Malagón offers more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1205946060/ascent-to-the-plaza-de-los-moros-via-the-sierra-de-malagon-circular-from-malagon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Plaza de los Moros via the Sierra de Malagón — circular from Malagón&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.9-mile (22.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain, often taking over six hours to complete.

Q: Are there trails with good viewpoints?
A: Yes, several trails offer scenic viewpoints, particularly those that ascend into the Sierra de Malagón or circle the reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3582372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight, providing expansive views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Carrión De Calatrava?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Carrión De Calatrava. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any natural springs or unique natural features to visit?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3495956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de los Hervideros&lt;/a&gt; are natural springs historically valued for their therapeutic properties. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3635697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Emperor's Baths&lt;/a&gt; are another natural monument worth exploring, often integrated into routes around the Embalse de Vicario.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Carrión De Calatrava?
A: The hiking trails in Carrión De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical intrigue.
tips: It was one of the earliest founded new cities of Al Andalus, becoming the Islamic capital of the region (854-1147), due to its privileged location next to the old main road that linked Córdoba with Toledo.

An important defensive system was the riverbed, with a much greater volume in the Middle Ages, which turned the place into a true island in the waters. The wall is surrounded by forty-four towers, and the unique coraches, designed to supply the city with water if it was surrounded.

Original headquarters of the Order of Calatrava, this ancient city-fortress was founded by the Umayyads at the end of the 8th century, being part of the territories of al-Andalus for four centuries. When it passed to Castile in the middle of the 12th century, it was one of the first possessions of the Order of the Temple. When it was abandoned by this order, it was the origin and first headquarters of the Order of Calatrava, until it was replaced by the castle of Calatrava la Nueva.

It is one of the most important archaeological sites of Islamic origin in the Iberian Peninsula. Of its defensive structure, its walls and moat stand out, as well as the watchtowers. The medina and the suburbs were the center of urban life within the walls, while the fortress housed the rooms of the leaders.

Beautiful setting next to the ruins of the Castle of Calatrava la Vieja. Here is the Virgin of the Incarnation, patron saint of Carrión, kept but in full view of all, until Easter Sunday, which is taken to the town of Carrión where it remains for 40 days

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Guadiana Natural Trail?
faqs.answer: The specific komoot tour, which is a circular route by the Río Guadiana, is approximately 10.3 kilometers (6.4 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes to complete. However, the full Guadiana Natural Trail (GR 114) is an extensive route spanning over 850 kilometers, so hiking time varies greatly depending on the section you choose.

faqs.question: Is the Guadiana Natural Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The difficulty of the Guadiana Natural Trail varies significantly by section. The komoot tour described is rated as 'moderate'. Some parts of the larger GR 114 are considered 'easy' with minimal elevation gain, while others are 'quite difficult' with steep ascents, descents, and dense vegetation, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. It's best to research the specific stage you plan to hike.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Guadiana Natural Trail?
faqs.answer: For the specific komoot tour, which starts near the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Encarnación, parking is generally available in the vicinity of the hermitage or nearby towns. For other sections of the extensive GR 114, access points and parking options will vary, often found at the start or end of specific stages in towns or designated trailheads.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the specific komoot tour, you can explore historical sites such as the Calatrava La Vieja archaeological site and its walls, as well as the Hermitage of the Virgin of the Encarnación. The broader Guadiana Natural Trail offers diverse landscapes, including lagoons, crop fields, wetlands, and historical elements like old railway tunnels and the medieval village of Mértola.

faqs.question: When is the best time to hike the Guadiana Natural Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Guadiana Natural Trail generally depends on the specific section and climate. Spring and autumn often offer milder temperatures, making them ideal for hiking. Summers can be very hot, especially in inland areas, while winters can be cooler and potentially wetter. Always check the local weather forecast before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Guadiana Natural Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many natural trails in Spain are dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary by municipality or protected area. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Guadiana Natural Trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, no permits or entrance fees are required to access the Guadiana Natural Trail itself, as it is a public natural trail. However, if you plan to visit specific archaeological sites, museums, or natural parks along the route, these may have their own entrance fees or require prior booking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on the Guadiana Natural Trail is highly varied. Some sections feature wide dirt tracks and flat areas, while others can be mountainous and sinuous with steep inclines and descents. You might encounter abundant vegetation, olive fields, meadows, and even old railway lines with trenches and tunnels. The specific komoot tour is rated 'moderate', suggesting a mix of manageable paths.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Guadiana Natural Trail (GR 114) intersects with and incorporates sections of several other paths. Notable intersecting trails include the Camino Natural del Guadiana, Ruta de los Yeseros. Cañada Real de la Plata "El Cordel", Camino de las carretas, and Ruta Virgen de la Encarnación.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The Guadiana Natural Trail, particularly within areas like the Guadiana Valley Natural Park, is known for its biodiversity. You might have opportunities for birdwatching, including various eagle species and storks (even black storks). Rare species like the Iberian lynx also inhabit the region, though sightings are uncommon.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation along the trail?
faqs.answer: As a long-distance trail, many stages of the Guadiana Natural Trail conclude in towns or villages that offer amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. It's recommended to plan your stages in advance and check for available services in the towns along your chosen route.

waypoints.name: Calatrava La Vieja archaeological site open to visitors
Hermitage of the Virgin of the Encarnación
